taxonomy: Macrosphyra paleacea is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is native to tropical Africa. The classification of Macrosphyra paleacea places it within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Sapindales, and family Sapindaceae. This species is further classified into the genus Macrosphyra and is often found growing in humid environments. The classification of Macrosphyra paleacea is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular analysis.
uses: Macrosphyra paleacea is traditionally used in some African cultures for its medicinal properties. Its bark, leaves, and roots are used to treat various ailments. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help with wound healing and infections. In traditional medicine, Macrosphyra paleacea is used to treat fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. However, its effectiveness and safety for human consumption have not been extensively scientifically studied.
